title: Insitu X Ray Photoemission Study of Nb Surface Under Plasma Cleaning During MID T BAKING For SRF Cavities
abstract: Specific heat treatments applied to superconducting radiofrequency (SRF) cavities, such as nitrogen infusion or Mid T baking, aim to improve the quality factor (Qo) at medium accelerating fields (10 to 20 MV/m). These treatments reduce the BCS surface resistance by tuning the mean free path of niobium over a few hundred na-nometers, either by diffusing oxygen from the native oxide layer or by diffusing nitrogen after the dissolution of the oxide layer. However, these treatments preclude the usual chemical polishing, as it would reverse the beneficial effects of the heat treatments, making the cavities highly sensitive to surface contamination. In particular, the formation of niobium carbides, which can mask the expected benefits, strongly depends on the annealing conditions, surface preparation, and the materials history. To better understand these phenomena, niobium samples was annealed under ultrahigh vacuum (Mid T baking) with Ar/O2 plasma treatment to investi-gate surface contamination with insitu heat treatment at 500 C and XPS analysis.
